rationale: Concrete economic noun with strong selectional restrictions; foils like *the price of the laughter* or agent-subject collocation violations are straightforward.
Monosemous concrete noun; clean selectional foils (e.g., *ate a photograph of*) and collocation foils (*a loud photograph*) both trivially available.
Concrete location noun with clear spatial/agricultural semantics; gairaigo garden is H2 test case so must be kept. Foils like *drank from the garden* work cleanly.

High polysemy (eye of storm, eye of needle, good eye, etc.). Metaphorical extensions are routine in English so non-body-part foils risk being rescued. Careful foil restricted to anatomical context required.
Metaphorical uses widespread (father of the nation, Father in religious sense). Careful to restrict to biological-parent-context foils. JP one_to_many but tested sense is core.

tested_sense_desc: part of the day with no sun
rationale: Temporal noun with clear selectional constraints (colocates with verbs of sleeping, darkness). Foils like *the night tasted sweet* or *measured the night in pounds* violate selection cleanly.

tested_sense_desc: brother of your mother or father
rationale: Monosemous kinship term; any selectional foil violating [+human, +male, +kin] works (*the uncle barked loudly*).
High polysemy (shopping center, center of attention, etc.) + one_to_many JP (gairaigo risk). Tested sense is spatial middle. Foils must avoid metaphorical rescue paths.
High polysemy (musical note, banknote, take note, note of something). The writing sense is one of several. Need to frame context tightly to lock sense.
Core kinship sense stable; religious/solidarity uses rare in everyday register. Foil violations of [+human, +male] straightforward.
Monosemous time unit; foils violating temporal/measurement semantics (*ate the hour*, *the hour is red*) are clean.
Although abstract, the sense is well-defined and selection restrictions allow clean foils (e.g., *cooked the plan in oil*, *the plan smelled salty*). Gairaigo but tested sense is core.

tested_sense_desc: green leaves that cover the ground
rationale: Concrete plant noun; foils like *drank the grass slowly* cleanly violate. Slang sense rare in Japan context.
Common concrete sense with strong colligation (take/have a bath, bathroom). Foils violating washing-venue semantics work.
Concrete body-part noun; shoulder-of-road is rare. Foils violating anatomical location (*the shoulder laid eggs*) are clean.
Monosemous activity verb; foil requiring [+agent, +animate] subject cleanly violated (*the chair prepared for*). Propositional object scope limited.
Monosemous human/emotive verb; selectional foils with inanimate subjects (*the rock laughed loudly*) work. Only risk is poetic personification but clean enough for 4-8 word foil.
Monosemous cognitive verb requiring [+animate, +cognizer] subject. Foils like *the window forgot* are clean violations.
High polysemy (machine works, plan works, work out). The employment sense is dominant but other readings rescue many foils. Must fix context with workplace/payment cues.
High polysemy (return a book, return a phone call, returns on investment). Motion sense must be locked by directional cues. Foils with non-location goals need care.
High polysemy + gairaigo (chekku) + one_to_many JP. Many readings (check a box, check in, restrain). Tight context needed; H2 test case so keep in.

tested_sense_desc: say or write an answer to somebody
rationale: Monosemous communication verb; foils requiring inanimate subject or non-communicative object (*the tree replied to the sound*) work.
Core transfer verb, abundant selectional foils (*the idea brought a book*). Minor metaphorical uses (brings joy) limited risk at 4-8 words.

tested_sense_desc: look at for a long time
rationale: Monosemous perceptual verb requiring [+animate, +eyes] subject. Foils like *the bottle stared at* are clean.
Relational/limiting adjective (only attributive + fixed collocations: main course, main road). Foil space narrow; need colligation-based b_collocation foil focus.
Extremely general evaluative adjective combines with nearly any noun. Selectional foils weak; collocational foils possible but hard to make CLEAN rather than just odd.
High polysemy (cold personality, cold shoulder, cold war, cold fact). Must lock thermal reading; foils like *a cold sound* rescue as synaesthesia.
Fairly narrow modal adjective but combines freely with abstract nouns (plan, answer). Foil space restricted; b_collocation with incompatible gradable contexts may work.

High polysemy (general as military rank, general as opposite of specific, general idea). one_to_many JP. Must lock adjective+usual sense; military noun rescue is a major risk for even clearly adjectival slots.
Intensifier/evaluative adjective combines freely. Similar to bad; CLEAN foil requires specific collocational violation since selection is permissive.
High polysemy (feature film, facial features, feature of product). Gairaigo + one_to_many JP. Need tight product/property context to fix sense.

tested_sense_desc: person who trains members of sports teams
rationale: High polysemy (coach = bus in BrE, coach class, personal coach). Loanword, H2 test case, retain with tight sports context cues.
Concrete plant noun; selectional foils with verbs incompatible with plants (*the weed called home*) work cleanly.
Concrete food noun; foils violating edibility/plant semantics clean.
Abstract noun with clear epistemic semantics; foils violating information-container semantics (*baked the knowledge*) work.
Mathematical sense well-constrained; foils with non-numeric contexts (*the average smelled good*) clean.
High polysemy (social circle, circle as verb, circle of friends). Gairaigo (saakuru = club in Japan). Need geometric context cues to isolate tested sense.
Concrete instrument noun; foils with incompatible functional contexts (*the knife sang loudly*) or abstract subjects clean.
Abstract legal noun but tested sense is central. Foils like *cooked the justice* or *the justice ran home* work. Personified justice (blind goddess) may need vetting.
High polysemy + very abstract meronymy. Combines with almost any whole-thing noun; selectional foils weak. b_collocation violations (section + verb of eating) may work.
Loanword + very general locative. Modern sense 'website' risks rescue of many foils. Tight physical-location cues needed. Keep for H2 but careful.
High polysemy (sheet of paper, sheet of ice, balance sheet). Loanword. Tested sense is bedding-specific. Context must include bed/bedroom cues; otherwise paper reading rescues.
Monosemous concrete noun; foils incompatible with paper-letter context (*fried the envelope alive*) clean.
High polysemy (cap of a bottle, spending cap, cap as verb). Gairaigo (kyappu). Tested sense is headwear; tight clothing context required.

tested_sense_desc: place to live inside a tall building
rationale: Monosemous dwelling noun; loanword (aapaato) but H2 case. Foils with incompatible predicate types (*the apartment barked*) clean.
Concrete physical action verb; foils with incompatible objects (*wrapped the idea in anger*) produce clean selectional violations.
Abstract giving verb; very general complement set (money, ideas, time). Foil space narrow. b_collocation with idiosyncratic PPs (*contribute at*) can work.
Monosemous search verb requiring [+animate, +intentional] subject. Foils like *the rain sought advice* are clean.
Meronymic verb with clear container-semantics. Foils violating containment relation (*the idea contained the box*) work.
Clear search activity; [+animate, +intentional] subject. Foils with inanimate subjects (*the wall searched for a clue*) clean.
Monosemous avoidance verb; requires [+animate, +intentional] subject. Foils such as *the puddle avoided me* workable.
Transfer verb with [+animate] recipient. Foils with inanimate recipients or abstract reception violations work.
Concrete contact verb; foils violating contact/gentleness semantics (*the concept bumped the floor*) clean.
Monosemous meronymic verb; foils violating part-whole schema (*the leaf included a song*) clean.

tested_sense_desc: feeling good about what you have done
rationale: Emotive predicate requiring [+animate, +cognizer] subject; foils like *the chair was proud* straightforward.
Very general evaluative + heavy gairaigo use. one_to_many JP. Selectional restrictions minimal; collocational foil possible but vague.
Aspectual adjective with clear semantics; foils with one-off events (*the constant accident last week*) create clean violations.
Physical decay adjective applied to food/wood/teeth etc. Foils with abstract or live-human nouns (*the rotten friendship smelled strange*) create clean selectional violations.
Texture adjective but extends broadly (smooth talker, smooth jazz, smooth transition). Gairaigo. Tactile sense must be forced with surface/touch cues.
Relational adjective with fixed colligations (junior staff, junior high). Loanword. Narrow foil space; b_collocation most promising.
Abstract but well-defined. Foils with inanimate-unsuitable actions (*the rock's behavior laughed*) clean.
Monosemous event noun; foils with incompatible predicates (*ate the celebration*) or solemn/negative contexts create clean violations.
Monosemous speech-act noun; foils with non-communicative verbs or incompatible agents work.
Linguistic noun with clear semantics; foils with incompatible verbs (*cooked a phrase*) clean. Musical phrase sense minor.

Pre-flagged. The lawyer/advocate sense dominates contemporary usage, and JP translation pulls toward kaunseru/counseling-specific registers. Tested guidance sense is minority and hard to lock in 4-8 words without triggering legal rescue or counseling-gairaigo rescue.
Abstract mass noun; foils with incompatible subjects/verbs (*the wealth barked*) clean. Metaphor 'wealth of knowledge' is at-risk but avoidable.
Monosemous domain noun; foils violating activity-type semantics (*eating agriculture*) clean.
Register-heavy (political); also 'diet regime' sense exists. Tested sense is political. Foils with incompatible predicates possible but register cues needed.
Human-referring noun; loanword, H2 case. Foils requiring [+human, +active, +helping] violated by inanimate subjects clean.
Monosemous abstract condition noun; foils like *drank the poverty* clean.
Cultural abstract noun; combines with genitive phrases. Distinguishing from 'history'/'legacy' in foil is subtle. b_collocation more tractable than a_selectional.
Business/finance sense dominant in modern usage; 'useful thing' sense is broader. Foils risk rescue by finance reading. Register cues matter.
Polysemy risk (CIA/military-intelligence sense). Cognitive sense tested. Tight educational/cognitive context cues needed.
High polysemy (fruit pit, mosh pit, orchestra pit, armpit). Must force ground-depression reading with spatial verbs. Foil risk from rescue.
Collective human noun; foils requiring inanimate or non-spectator subjects (*the audience rusted*) clean.

Pre-flagged. Formal/legal/medical register limits natural 4-8 word frames; in casual short sentences 'consent' sounds stiff or pulls toward consent-form/sexual-consent framings. Many foils either collapse to near-synonym of 'agree' (no clean violation) or trigger register incongruity rather than semantic error.

Pre-flagged. Report verb accepts wide propositional/nominal complements. Foils possible with inanimate subjects (*the door specified the time*) but collocational foils are harder since 'specify X' admits almost any X. Lean on a_selectional.

Pre-flagged. 'Exhibit symptoms/behavior' sense very common, rescues many foils. Tested sense is public display. Need museum/gallery cues in context; a_selectional with inanimate exhibitor subject can work.
High polysemy (capture an image, capture attention, capture a city). Gairaigo. Physical-catching sense must be anchored with animate prey + chaser.
Monosemous continuation verb requiring [+agent, +animate] or [+process]. Foils with wrong aspectual frames or impossible subjects (*the stone proceeded to sleep deeply*) work.

Pre-flagged (spec §5.4.1 explicit). Accepts virtually any propositional/abstract object; inanimate-agent foils like *the weather debated the plan* are routinely rescued as personification. No 4-8 word context can force a clean selectional violation without sounding deliberately metaphorical.

Pre-flagged. Aspectual continuation verb. Subjects can be eventualities/conditions/people. Selectional foils possible with punctual-bounded events (*the explosion persisted briefly*) but near-synonymy with 'continue' narrows clean-foil space.
Requires quantifiable/measurable complement. Foils with non-quantifiable objects (*exceeded the sunrise*) create clean selectional violations.

Pre-flagged (spec §5.4.1 explicit). Dense layered polysemy: physical absorption, absorbing information, absorbing costs, being absorbed in a task. Almost any selectional foil collapses to a well-attested metaphorical reading. 4-8 word foils cannot cleanly exclude all other readings.
Pre-flagged. Gradable modifier of numerical/measurable quantities; near-synonymy with 'rough', 'about'. b_collocation foils with non-quantitative nouns (*an approximate emotion*) possible but subtle.
Attributive-only relational adjective with fixed colligations (prior knowledge, prior experience). Foil space narrow; b_collocation with incongruent post-modification is best route.

Aspectual frequency adjective; foils with punctual/one-off event nouns (*a frequent accident yesterday*) create clean violations. Distinct from 'frequent' as verb (visit) but verb reading rare in adjective slot.
Two sub-senses: stable/invariant and logically compatible ('consistent with'). Tested sense is stability. b_collocation foils with variable-event nouns work.

Pre-flagged (spec §5.4.1 explicit). Attributive-only, fixed colligational patterns (former president, the former), and competes with 'former' as part of 'the former...the latter' anaphor. In 4-8 word frames the adjective reduces to a handful of well-formed templates; foils either violate syntax unnaturally or remain acceptable via familiar template.
Requires reciprocal/plural referents (mutual friends, mutual respect). Foils with singular or non-relational nouns (*a mutual stone*) may create violations but verges on collocation oddness.
Concrete biochemical noun; loanword with H2 value. Foils with non-nutritive contexts (*he wore a vitamin*) clean.

tested_sense_desc: opening in the ground or in the side of a hill
rationale: Concrete geological noun; foils violating spatial/dimensional semantics (*he drank the cave*) clean.

Pre-flagged (spec §5.4.1 explicit). Extreme referential promiscuity - any abstract situation or hypothetical can be a 'scenario'. Combined with loanword shinario (screenplay) rescue, few foils survive. Even *fried the scenario* reads as idiomatic.
Concrete substance noun; soap opera sense rare in 4-8 word contexts. Foils with incompatible functional contexts (*read the soap quietly*) clean.
Human profession noun; foils requiring [+human, +teaching] role violated by inanimate subjects (*the bookshelf tutored the students*) work.
Concrete gem noun; loanword with H2 value. Baseball diamond/card suit rare in default reading. Foils like *the diamond barked* clean.

tested_sense_desc: group of people who play music
rationale: Monosemous collective noun; loanword with H2 value. Foils with non-musical contexts (*fed the orchestra grass*) clean.
High polysemy (time slot, slot machine, slot in schedule). Loanword. Physical aperture sense must be locked with insertion context cues.
Polysemy (scrap metal, scrap of information, scrap = fight). Gairaigo. Tested sense is physical fragment. Foils must fix physical-piece reading.
Concrete surface-structure noun (bread crust, Earth's crust). Both readings share 'hard outside part' semantics so tested sense robust. Foils violating hard-exterior semantics clean.
Legal/evaluative noun; foils with incompatible predicates (*cooked a verdict*) or non-deliberative agents clean.
Monosemous event-location noun; foils with non-event contexts or incompatible predicates (*ate the venue*) clean.
Monosemous institutional-building noun; foils with incompatible predicates (*the embassy sang a song*) clean.

tested_sense_desc: plant that is smoked in cigarettes
rationale: Concrete plant/product noun; dual loanword. Foils incompatible with smokable-material contexts (*the tobacco danced*) clean.
Concrete urban-location noun; bowling alley sense minor. Foils with incompatible predicates (*the alley ate lunch*) clean.
Monosemous prohibition verb requiring [+animate, +authority] subject. Foils with inanimate subjects (*the mountain forbade the meeting*) create clean violations.
Concrete dimension-change verb; 'shrink' as noun (therapist) rare. Foils with incompatible subjects (*the thought shrank the lake*) clean.
Physical action verb requiring [+agent, +animate]. Foils with inanimate throwers (*the lamp flung the ball*) clean.

Monosemous emotional verb requiring [+animate, +sentient] subject. Foils with incompatible subjects (*the stone wept softly*) work but personification rescue possible at poetic register; tightly literal context needed.
Formal report verb; requires [+animate, +intentional] subject. Foils with inanimate subjects (*the book disclosed the plan*) create clean violations.
Technical action verb. Gairaigo partial. Foils with incompatible objects (*activated the breeze*, *activated the breakfast*) clean.
Clear physical process verb; emotional metaphor (explode with joy) limited risk in literal context. Foils with incompatible subjects (*the silence exploded the chair*) clean.
Scalar-change verb; abstract uses common but concrete-dimension reading robust. Foils with incompatible dimension arguments work.
Specialized action verb (medical/horticultural); foils with inappropriate themes (*transplanted the music to a vase*) clean.
Spatial relational adjective; takes locative arguments. Foils with non-locatable subjects (*adjacent opinion*) create clean violations. Narrow colligations help b_collocation foils too.
Requires [+animate, +mental] subject. Foils with inanimate subjects (*a sane desk*) cleanly violate.
Speed adjective; swift-the-bird sense is a noun so doesn't rescue adjective slots. Foils with non-temporal nouns (*a swift weight*) or incompatible aspect clean.
Evaluative adjective for propositional/source reliability; foils with physical-only nouns (*a credible rock*) violate selection.
Concrete texture adjective; foils with incompatible nouns (*a greasy idea*) cleanly violate. Metaphorical 'greasy smile' possible but avoidable.
Evaluative deviation adjective; foils with inherently-fixed-value nouns or mismatched scales (*an abnormal number three*) create violations.
Monosemous concrete body-appendage noun; foils with incompatible locations (*the mustache on her shoe*) cleanly violate.
Evaluative place noun; foils with incompatible predicates or horrific-context nouns cleanly violate. Religious sense compatible with tested sense.
Concrete food noun; foils with incompatible functional contexts (*the pastry drove the car*) clean. no_direct JP indicates need for clear food-context cues.
Monosemous container noun; foils with incompatible contents or verbs (*ate a vase for dinner*) clean.

tested_sense_desc: group of musical notes that are played at the same time
rationale: Musical sense dominant; 'strike a chord' metaphor avoidable in concrete musical context. Foils with non-musical predicates (*cooked the chord*) clean.

tested_sense_desc: shape with two long and two short sides
rationale: Monosemous geometric noun; foils with incompatible verbs (*the rectangle sang*) cleanly violate.
Concrete fruit noun; lime (chemical)/lime color sense minor in 4-8 word frames. Foils with incompatible predicates (*the lime argued*) clean.
Concrete sound noun; foils with non-auditory contexts (*ate the hum*) clean. Verb sense distinct and unlikely to interfere.
Concrete food noun; foils with incompatible verbs (*the pork drove home*) clean.

tested_sense_desc: liquid that is made to smell nice
rationale: Concrete liquid-product noun; foils with incompatible verbs (*read the perfume*) clean.
Human fantasy-role noun; metaphorical 'computer wizard' possible but avoidable with fantasy context. Foils with incompatible predicates clean.
Concrete place noun with religious/conservation subsenses sharing 'safe place' schema. Foils with incompatible predicates (*ate a sanctuary*) clean.
Monosemous measurement noun (esp. vertical); foils with horizontal measurements or incompatible subjects (*the cup's altitude*) clean.
Concrete clothing noun; loanword. Foils with incompatible contexts (*the robe drank coffee*) clean.
Human-referring noun with clear concrete sense; 'software pirate' secondary. Foils with incompatible predicates (*the pirate bloomed*) clean.
Formal communication verb requiring [+animate] subject. Foils with inanimate subjects (*the wall notified us*) clean.
Motion verb requiring [+animate, +intentional] subject. Foils with inanimate subjects (*the bookshelf intruded*) clean.
Physical/abstract removal verb; foils with incompatible themes (*erased the thunder*) clean.

tested_sense_desc: cut or tear into small pieces
rationale: Physical destruction verb; foils with incompatible themes (*shredded the music into loud pieces*) clean.
Termination verb with clear argument types (contracts, subscriptions, life). Foils with incompatible subjects (*the rock expired yesterday morning*) clean.
Cognitive verb requiring [+animate, +cognizer]. Foils with inanimate subjects (*the chair meditated quietly*) clean.
Combustion verb with clear physical semantics; metaphorical 'ignite passion' separable. Foils with incompatible themes (*ignited the lake*) clean.
Exchange verb requiring [+animate, +two-party] structure. Foils with single-party configurations or non-exchangeable themes work.
Physical penetration verb; foils with non-penetrable themes or impossible instruments clean (*pierced the song with a thought*).
Monosemous state adjective; foils with chaotic-event nouns (*a tranquil explosion*) cleanly violate.
Concrete physical adjective applied to hair-bearing entities. Foils with hair-incapable subjects (*a bald refrigerator*) cleanly violate. Metaphor 'bald statement' minor.
Physical moisture adjective; foils with inherently-dry abstract nouns (*a moist idea*) cleanly violate.
Applied mainly to animals/situations. Foils with inanimate non-domesticable subjects (*a tame rock*) or incompatible gradations cleanly violate.

Pre-flagged (spec §5.4.1 explicit). Relational adjective with narrow colligations (mandatory attendance, mandatory training) and register-bound to legal/institutional discourse. Foil inventory tiny; any colligational violation tends to read as merely unusual collocation rather than a clean semantic error.
Evaluative adjective requiring [+animate, +cognizer] subject or deliberative-action nouns. Foils with inanimate subjects (*a prudent rock*) or non-decisional events cleanly violate.
